behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` does not match raw body; do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at handler->queue boundary, not just HMAC math. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a unit test of `verifySignature()` can pass while handler code still enqueues before/after sending 401 due to wiring bug. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process HTTP handler + fake/in-memory queue can reproduce. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real network failure semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Start app with test queue adapter that records writes. Send request with valid body and invalid signature header. Assert HTTP 401 and queue write count stays 0.

behaviors.description: Write event to persistent queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): unit-testing handler response code alone misses bug where `res.send(200)` happens before awaited queue write promise resolves. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store (e.g., sqlite/file-backed queue) is enough; no real external network/subprocess needed. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): none strong for System unless behavior demanded OS crash/durability guarantees; text does not. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior says 'written before 200', not 'durably fsynced across crash'.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real queue module configured to local test DB/file. Instrument timestamps/order (or blocking hook) so queue `enqueue` completion is observable. Assert 200 is emitted only after enqueue success is recorded.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is async handoff behavior between handler and processor worker. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): unit test of processor or handler in isolation misses bug where handler accidentally awaits `processEvent()` promise. REAL-INFRA: deterministic delayed downstream stub inside process is sufficient; no real OS/network required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real external infrastructure is not required to expose accidental await/blocking.
behaviors.test_description: Configure downstream processor to sleep (e.g., 10s) after queue acceptance. Send valid webhook and assert response returns quickly while processor completion happens later.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even when earlier events are slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is a real-time SLO under concurrency; requires real HTTP server/event-loop scheduling and wall-clock measurement. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): unit tests cannot reveal queue contention/event-loop starvation under concurrent requests. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): in-process mocked transport or fake timers can pass while real server misses 3s due to socket backlog, middleware limits, or event-loop blocking. INTEGRATION-BRAKE forces upgrade on (a)=yes. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML decision path exists in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run service as real process, pre-load slow-processing events, then fire concurrent valid webhooks over real HTTP (loopback). Measure end-to-end latency per response; assert each ack <=3s under defined concurrency.

behaviors.description: Do not write event to queue if event ID was already received.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness depends on handler+queue/store interaction (ID check/unique constraint), not only local function logic. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a pure dedupe function can pass while integration bug still inserts duplicate due to wrong key mapping or non-atomic check-then-insert. REAL-INFRA: local persistent DB/file queue is enough; no external OS/network behavior needed beyond local module wiring. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require distributed/networked dedupe semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use real queue/store implementation in test mode. Post same event ID twice (sequential and optionally concurrent). Assert first is accepted and second is not enqueued; queue contains single record for that ID.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and include `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is mapping of queue-capacity error to HTTP contract across module boundary. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): unit test of error-to-status mapper can pass while handler fails to propagate real `QueueFull` error class and returns 500. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required; queue module configured to full state in-process is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real external infrastructure is unnecessary to trigger full-queue branch.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue with tiny capacity and fill it, or stub queue module to emit canonical full error. Send another webhook; assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header present with expected value/format.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects writes beyond limit.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates queue implementation with its persistence layer/config wiring, not just a single pure function. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): unit test of counter logic misses real bug where DB query/filter miscounts pending items and allows overflow. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue test is sufficient; no real external network/subprocess required. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): none concrete for System unless behavior required OS disk-full semantics; text specifies configured logical capacity, not physical resource exhaustion. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected based on that disqualifying text.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with capacity N in test storage. Attempt N+1 enqueues and assert first N succeed, N+1 fails with queue-full rejection type used by handler path.
